steps.title: Identify what the passage establishes
steps.content: The passage describes Alegado and colleagues' research on S. rosetta colony formation. The key finding is that Alegado's team concluded neither V. fischeri nor B. fragilis played a significant role in multicellular development — meaning neither bacterium reliably triggered colony formation in S. rosetta.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — S. rosetta tended to form colonies when exposed to V. fischeri and when exposed to B. fragilis.: Both bacteria triggered colony formation — this contradicts the conclusion that neither played a significant role. ✗
  • Choice B — S. rosetta tended to form colonies when exposed to V. fischeri, but not when exposed to B. fragilis.: Only one bacterium triggered colonies — still contradicts ruling out both bacteria's significance. ✗
  • Choice C — S. rosetta tended to form colonies when exposed to B. fragilis, but not when exposed to V. fischeri.: Only B. fragilis triggered colonies — still contradicts the conclusion that neither was significant. ✗
  • Choice D — S. rosetta did not tend to form colonies when exposed to either V. fischeri or B. fragilis.: Neither bacterium triggered colony formation — this directly supports the conclusion that neither played a significant role. ✓
That matches Choice D — S. rosetta did not tend to form colonies when exposed to either V. fischeri or B. fragilis..
